title: Bymovirus Diversity in Barley Crops in France from 2013 to 2016
author: Rolland, Mathieu
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: A study was undertaken to determine the distribution and diversity of viruses causing yellow mosaic disease in barley in France. 241 samples from symptomatic susceptible,  rym4  and  rym5  varieties were gathered. The viruses present in all samples were identified by specific RT-PCR assays and, for selected samples, by double stranded RNA metagenomic analyses.
steps_raw: A study was undertaken to determine the distribution and diversity of viruses causing yellow mosaic disease in barley in France. 241 samples from symptomatic susceptible,  rym4  and  rym5  varieties were gathered. The viruses present in all samples were identified by specific RT-PCR assays and, for selected samples, by double stranded RNA metagenomic analyses.
keywords: Virology, ecology and epidemiology, genetics and resistance, barley, BaMMV, BaYMV, soil-borne virus occurrence in France
